medialive.channel.CfnAbout:
The AWS::MediaLive::Channel resource is a MediaLive resource type that creates a channel.

A MediaLive channel ingests and transcodes (decodes and encodes) source content from the inputs that are attached to that channel, and packages the new content into outputs.

medialive.channel.CdiInputSpecification.Description: Specification of CDI inputs for this channel.

medialive.channel.ChannelClass.Description: The class for this channel. For a channel with two pipelines, the class is STANDARD. For a channel with one pipeline, the class is SINGLE_PIPELINE.

medialive.channel.Destinations.Description: The settings that identify the destination for the outputs in this MediaLive output package.

medialive.channel.EncoderSettings.Description: The encoding configuration for the output content.

medialive.channel.InputAttachments.Description: The list of input attachments for the channel.

medialive.channel.InputSpecification.Description: The input specification for this channel. It specifies the key characteristics of the inputs for this channel: the maximum bitrate, the resolution, and the codec.

medialive.channel.LogLevel.Description: The verbosity for logging activity for this channel. Charges for logging (which are generated through Amazon CloudWatch Logging) are higher for higher verbosities.

medialive.channel.Name.Description: A name for this audio selector. The AudioDescription (in an output) references this name in order to identify a specific input audio to include in that output.

medialive.channel.RoleArn.Description: The IAM role for MediaLive to assume when running this channel. The role is identified by its ARN.

medialive.channel.Tags.Description: A collection of tags for this channel. Each tag is a key-value pair.

medialive.channel.Vpc.Description: Settings to enable VPC mode in the channel, so that the endpoints for all outputs are in your VPC.
